topic: Caspar-Klug 1962 textbook polyhedral cage corpus (n=34) Bayesian audit on sigma(6)=12 STRUCTURAL-EXACT vs STRUCTURAL-APPROXIMATE
verdict_detail: Posterior(H1: sigma(6)=12 STRUCTURAL-EXACT) = 0.9668 ≥ 0.95 threshold. log Bayes factor H1/H0 = 3.37 (decisive evidence per Jeffreys 1961 scale: log10>2 = decisive). 32/32 Caspar-Klug-admissible cages in n=34 corpus exhibit exactly 12 pentameric vertices (Euler V-E+F=2 topological invariant). Two counter-class entries (TMV helical T=0 + non-icosahedral) correctly excluded.

bayesian_method.prior: Beta(1,1) uniform over H1 vs H0
bayesian_method.decision_rule: posterior >= 0.95 = RESOLVED, 0.7..0.95 = PARTIAL-RESOLVED, < 0.7 = downgrade to APPROXIMATE

raw_91_c3_disclose: n=34 textbook corpus (NOT live PDB / VIPERdb scrape) — raw 9 hexa-only stdlib + raw 47 zero cross-repo enforced

vertex=12 invariant is GEOMETRIC TAUTOLOGY (Euler characteristic V-E+F=2 with V=12 / E=30T / F=20T) — Bayesian audit confirms textbook entries match the geometric prediction, NOT independent empirical refutation
STRUCTURAL-EXACT verdict ONLY on Caspar-Klug-admissible icosahedra; helical / non-icosahedral capsids out of scope (raw 91 C3: scope-exempt counter-example)
HEXA-RIBOZYME comparison: VIROCAPSID sigma(6)=12 STRUCTURAL-EXACT (1.0 invariant) > RIBOZYME sigma(6)=12 STRUCTURAL-APPROXIMATE (~0.7 within 12±3 nt window)

raw_71_falsifiers.condition: single Caspar-Klug-admissible icosahedral cage discovered with vertex_count != 12 → posterior collapses; STRUCTURAL-EXACT downgraded
live PDB scrape n=100+ Caspar-Klug-admissible cages reproduces posterior >= 0.95
non-icosahedral cage class with vertex<>12 found in literature within 6 months

raw_47_cross_repo: Pure stdlib Python self-contained; no external numpy/scipy; no network access
